parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: false, rejected: true, reason: $('Node 2 — Guardrails Input Check').item.json.reason || 'Submission did not pass validation.' }) }}

parameters.text:
={{ 'Contract description:
' + $('Node 1 — Webhook Trigger').item.json.body.description + '

Extracted fields:
' + JSON.stringify($('Node 4b — Parse extracted JSON').item.json) + '

Filename: ' + ($('Node 1 — Webhook Trigger').item.json.body.filename || 'submission.txt') }}

options.systemMessage:
You are a senior compliance analyst for a regulatory audit platform (reference architecture: tools on AI Agent).

You have exactly TWO tools: rag_query and langgraph_agent. Do not call any other tool.

Tool order:
1. rag_query — Call FIRST with NO parameters: {}. The contract text is pre-loaded. Returns matches[] and insight. Use matches to fill similar_regulations and rag_insight in your final JSON.
2. langgraph_agent — Call ONCE after rag_query with NO parameters: {}. The request body is pre-built. Returns findings, overall_risk, and report_markdown.

If a tool returns an error, you may retry it once. After langgraph_agent returns, map its response into your answer. Produce ONLY one JSON object (no markdown fences):
{
  "contract_type": string,
  "routing_decision": "escalation" | "reviewer_queue" | "auto_file",
  "jurisdiction": string,
  "parties": [string],
  "overall_risk": "High" | "Medium" | "Low",
  "findings": [{ "contract_clause_id", "matched_regulatory_source", "matched_regulatory_article", "verdict", "risk", "justification", "confidence" }],
  "similar_regulations": [string],
  "rag_insight": string,
  "enrichment_notes": string,
  "confidence": number
}

routing_decision: escalation if overall_risk is High; reviewer_queue if Medium; auto_file if Low.
Cite only regulations from rag_query / langgraph_agent — do not invent sources.

parameters.toolDescription: Queries the regulatory RAG knowledge base (Layer 3, port 8001). Returns matches[] with source, article, score and an insight string. Call this BEFORE langgraph_agent. No parameters required — the contract text is pre-loaded from the webhook.
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ text: String($('Node 1 — Webhook Trigger').item.json.body.description || ''), top_k: 5 }) }}

parameters.toolDescription: Segments a PDF or document into contract clauses. Parameters: contract_id, filename, document_b64 (base64-encoded file).

Runs the LangGraph compliance audit (Layer 3, port 8004). Pass the 'input' argument set to the string 'ok'; the request body is pre-built from the workflow. Returns overall_risk, findings[], report_markdown, trace.
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ audit_id: 'aud_' + $execution.id, contract_id: 'ctr_' + $execution.id, clauses: [{ id: 'clause_1', contract_id: 'ctr_' + $execution.id, section: 'Body', text: String($('Node 1 — Webhook Trigger').item.json.body.description || ''), clause_type: 'other' }], jurisdiction: String(($('Node 4b — Parse extracted JSON').item.json.output || {}).jurisdiction || 'unknown'), contract_type: String(($('Node 4b — Parse extracted JSON').item.json.output || {}).contract_type || 'other') }) }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ success: false, human_review_required: true, message: 'Automated output checks flagged this submission for human review before it can be released as a final compliance result. The optional human-review webhook may not be configured yet - this response is returned to the Web UI instead.', flag_reason: $('Node 7 — Guardrails Output Check').item.json.reason || null, report: $('Node 6c — Parse report JSON').item.json }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, channel: 'escalation', report: $('Node 6c — Parse report JSON').item.json }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, channel: 'reviewer_queue', report: $('Node 6c — Parse report JSON').item.json }) }}
={{ JSON.stringify({ success: true, channel: 'auto_file', report: $('Node 6c — Parse report JSON').item.json }) }}
